prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='svr', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.69) and high seasonality variance (0.72), indicating models capture patterns very differently. svr leads trend tracking while NaiveSeasonal excels at seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost), supporting per-horizon selection. Given the significant disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_svr", "svr", "rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "THETA", "FT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ETS"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6907,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.69) and high seasonality variance (0.72), indicating models capture patterns very differently. svr leads trend tracking while NaiveSeasonal excels at seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost), supporting per-horizon selection. Given the significant disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"shrinkage": 0.35}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.388 (≥0.25) justifies robust combination approaches like dba_combination. RMSE spread between best (ARIMA: 1643.5) and worst (ONLY_DWT_catboost: 2995.3) is ~82%, favoring top-k selection over full mean. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.72)."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). All candidates are from valid_candidate_names. No leakage detected - inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. n_windows=3 meets threshold for weighted candidates. Universe leaderboards show current candidates perform well (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 is best overall with score 0.840, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 is second at 0.840). No pruning needed as we have <4 of any single type. All metrics covered adequately.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.69) and high seasonality variance (0.72), indicating models capture patterns very differently. svr leads trend tracking while NaiveSeasonal excels at seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost), supporting per-horizon selection. Given the significant disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.388 (≥0.25) justifies robust combination approaches like dba_combination. RMSE spread between best (ARIMA: 1643.5) and worst (ONLY_DWT_catboost: 2995.3) is ~82%, favoring top-k selection over full mean. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.72).

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). All candidates are from valid_candidate_names. No leakage detected - inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. n_windows=3 meets threshold for weighted candidates. Universe leaderboards show current candidates perform well (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 is best overall with score 0.840, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 is second at 0.840). No pruning needed as we have <4 of any single type. All metrics covered adequately.
